comments_for_c1: There were multiple delays throughout the build-out process of the platform, which significantly impacted our planned launch timeline. At several critical milestones, the platform was not fully developed, tested, or operational when required. As a result, it was not ready to be launched as a stable, fully functional service according to the agreed schedule. These delays affected deployment readiness, limited our ability to onboard users as planned, and created setbacks in meeting program objectives.
comments_for_c3: Following the launch of the platform, we encountered numerous system bugs that required immediate attention and resolution before the platform could be used as originally intended. These technical issues affected core functionality, limited usability, and required additional time and resources to diagnose and correct. As a result, progress slowed considerably, creating a bottleneck that impacted multiple components of our program and delayed operational workflows.
comments_for_d1: During the development of our analytical datasets, multiple errors were identified that required extensive troubleshooting and correction. Despite our team providing clear, accurate, and consistent feedback, it took several weeks to resolve these issues. These recurring errors and extended correction periods also required additional staff time and follow-up, further impacting overall program efficiency.
comments_for_c5: There is a noticeable lag between entering a case into our primary system and seeing that case reflected in the case-matching system. Currently, it takes approximately 24 hours for a newly entered case to appear. This delay presents operational challenges, particularly when same-day matching is required. The lag limits our ability to respond quickly, reduces efficiency in time-sensitive situations, and can slow down service delivery. Reducing this synchronization delay would significantly improve workflow efficiency and support more timely case matching.  Additionally, the bulk upload tool experienced significant and prolonged functionality issues. For several months, our state partner was unable to access or use the tool as intended. This prevented the timely submission and processing of critical data from an important state partner, further compounding program delays. The inability to receive and integrate this data disrupted reporting timelines, hindered informed decision-making, and placed additional strain on coordination and communication between teams. Together, these system limitations have had a measurable impact on overall program effectiveness and performance.
comments_for_c6: We have not yet experienced a surge response scenario; but they do load during routine tasks
comments_for_d2: Data products were developed and delivered incrementally over several years, resulting in a piecemeal and inefficient process. At various points, certain reports or data tools were available while others were still pending, limiting our ability to access a complete and cohesive set of information. This staggered rollout created gaps in reporting capabilities, required workarounds to bridge missing components, and reduced overall efficiency in meeting program and stakeholder needs.
comments_for_e2: I have developed several products within 1CDP; however, much of the training available on the platform was not aligned with my specific areas of expertise or the types of products I was building. As a result, I had to spend additional time searching for relevant guidance and resources to understand how the system functions and how to effectively leverage its features to meet my program’s specific needs. The lack of targeted, role specific training extended the learning curve and slowed overall development and implementation efforts.
comments_for_e3: Over the past few months, we have had to submit several support tickets, and response times for certain technical issues have been slower than anticipated. While we understand that staffing, time, and resource constraints may impact turnaround times, the delays have had operational consequences.  In one instance, it took several months to grant a state partner access to the system so they could begin submitting required data. This extended delay not only hindered timely data collection and reporting but also created understandable frustration for our state partner. Prolonged access issues such as this can strain partnerships and disrupt program timelines, particularly when external stakeholders rely on timely system functionality to fulfill their responsibilities.
comments_for_g1: While the platform offers potential, ongoing issues such as system delays, limited role-specific training, incomplete or inconsistent data products, and slow technical support—have hindered my ability to fully leverage it for program needs. These challenges have affected efficiency, timely data access, and overall usability, making it difficult to consider the implementation fully effective at this time.
comments_for_h1: It is still too early to determine whether 1CDP will improve data governance, as the platform was only recently launched to state partners at the end of last year. Further time and usage will be needed to assess its impact on data quality, consistency, and overall governance practices.
comments_for_h2: We have not had an outbreak response yet.
comments_for_h3: 1CDP does not currently enhance shared situational awareness internally. Many team members either do not use the platform or are reluctant to engage with it, largely because it is difficult to navigate. This limited adoption reduces the platform’s effectiveness as a centralized tool for collaboration and information sharing, hindering visibility across teams and impacting coordinated decision making.
comments_for_h4: Same as the comment in H3
i1: ["Platform Readiness and Launch Delays","System Bugs and Functional Issues","Data Processing Delays","Bulk Upload Tool Limitations","Performance and System Load Concerns","Incomplete or Inconsistent Data Products","Training Gaps","Limited Technical Support and Access Delays","Low Internal Adoption"]
i6: ["System Performance","Usability and Navigation","Data Accessibility and Consistency"]
i2: ["Centralized Data Platform","Enhanced Analytical Capabilities","Improved Tracking and Case Management","Foundational Infrastructure for Future Enhancements","Increased Transparency and Accountability"]
i7: While 1CDP has the potential to centralize data and support analytical and operational needs, several challenges have limited its effectiveness to date. System performance issues, limited training, and usability challenges have slowed adoption and impacted efficiency. Data accessibility and consistency remain ongoing concerns, particularly for external partners relying on timely submissions. Addressing these issues through targeted training, improved technical support, optimized tools, and more intuitive workflows would significantly enhance the platform’s value and usability. Continued engagement with users and state partners will be critical to ensuring the system meets program needs and supports timely, data driven decision making.
i3: To improve our program’s experience with 1CDP, several changes would be highly beneficial. Reducing system lag and improving overall performance would allow for more efficient workflows, including same day case matching availability. Enhanced, role specific training and easily accessible resources would help users quickly understand how to leverage the platform for program specific needs. Faster technical support and more timely resolution of issues would minimize delays and prevent frustration for both staff and state partners. Ensuring the bulk upload tool functions reliably and is accessible to partners would improve timely data submission and reporting. Additionally, making the platform more intuitive and easier to navigate would encourage broader internal adoption, improving shared situational awareness and collaboration across teams. Finally, delivering complete, consistent, and accurate data products along with clear processes for monitoring data quality and governance would increase efficiency and confidence in the information used to guide program decisions.
proficiency_level: Advanced- I build, modify, or manage workflows, pipelines, or structured processes (i.e. use code workbook, build workshops, etc)
functional_categ: ["Case Data Collection & Technical Assistance (i.e. CRFs, direct uploads, bulk upload tools, event forms, phone/call/inquiry/consultation logs, Case-lab matching, Case-case matching, reconciliation modules)","Situational Awareness & Dashboards (i.e. Situational Awareness dashboards, Data quality dashboards, Program management modules, Case approval dashboards)","Laboratory Data (i.e. ELIMS or LRN data, Commercial Laboratory feeds etc.)"]
i5: The primary training gaps that need to be addressed relate to the lack of role specific guidance and resources within 1CDP. Much of the available training does not align with the specific tasks or areas of expertise required by different users, making it difficult to quickly understand how to navigate the system and apply its features effectively. Users often need to spend additional time searching for relevant information or figuring out workflows independently. Providing targeted, practical training that reflects real world use cases, step by step guidance for key tools like the pipeline builder, codebook, bulk upload function, and examples tailored to program specific needs would significantly improve users’ confidence and efficiency in using the platform. Additionally, ongoing refresher training and easy to access support resources could help reinforce learning and ensure consistent adoption across teams.

branch_name: Poxvirus and Rabies Branch (PRB)- Mpox Workspace
comments_for_c2: This is a very complicated system and takes an incredibly long time to get simple answers (e.g., the question "how many cases were reported today", takes an inordinate amount of time to complete). These types of very basic queries should be a simple function. Data is never linked, so is frequently unreliable (e.g., our lab data was promised to be linked with epi data, it never is leading to significant communication/knowledge gaps and incorporating significant staff time to match samples with epi data).  System outages do not delay completing tasks because most of us have to download the data to do the tasks. Therefore, when the system is down, we have a backup. Analyses of up to date data becomes a problem though.
comments_for_c3: Functionally, we have not ever used this system when there was not a bug/glitch or something else going on. When we put tickets for a fix, it takes months to be addressed, leading to workarounds outside the system. Significant lags in deploying our clinical consult module led to significant losses in data and reporting multiple times throughout the year. We are unable to give simple answers to questions such as "how many severe cases do we have". I have to dig through email because the reporting system was offline for much of last year.
comments_for_c5: We had significant back and forth from our STLT partners that the data upload feature doesn't always work. Additionally, we have to keep backups of our data constantly in case an upload causes negative effects on existing data
comments_for_c6: Dashboards were developed during response, we have not developed dashboards due to data and other issues.
comments_for_d2: Without a system that works well to analyze data quickly/accurately, these systems are not timely to support decision making. The systems need to be designed in a way so that our epi staff can analyze our data in real time to get answers. During response this was possible with a large team, however, as an epi team with 2 permanent FTEs for the branch, this is far too complicated.
comments_for_d3: While they are trusted by leadership, partners often have more questions as it does not always correlate to their data
comments_for_e3: As noted in other questions, after putting in tickets to have simple fixes to workflows adjusted, it took months. The fixes were not sufficient for what was asked. Since it took months to get something resolved from the ticket, staff are demoralized to get anything fixed. It takes too long for nothing to be fixed so staff will perform workarounds instead.
comments_for_f1: Forceably yes it is. However, we are trying to find ways to remove it from routine operations since other platforms are better and easier to use
comments_for_f2: If the system were reliable, easy to use, good, real-time technical support, this would be possible, unfortunatley, workarounds are necessary for mission-critical workflows.
comments_for_f3: We are currently transitioning to using alternative systems because of how difficult 1CDP has been.
comments_for_f4: Would strongly urge CDC to move towards a different data platform.
comments_for_g1: 1CDP was implemented during a response. Unfortunately, this led to many cooks in the kitchen who are no longer with CDC, or outside our program. Therefore, 1CDP does not fulfill the needs of the program and has added many layers of complexity.
comments_for_g2: 1CDP has caused more confusion, more disconnectedness, and more time to do tasks. It is easier to use a simple excel sheet than use 1CDP.
comments_for_g3: As highlighted in multiple responses, the amount of time, training, finicking, and working with the platform, does not justify its value. Our old ACCESS based system is more functional and we have strongly considered going back to this system over 1CDP.
comments_for_h1: Due to necessary workarounds, this does not improve data governance.
comments_for_h5: Given how cumbersome 1CDP is, and how unlinked our data are, we have to perform workflows in R and other external programs anyway. 1CDP had no part in standardization.
comments_for_h6: R is a much more useful tool for auditing/tracing workflows than 1CDP
comments_for_h7: As our data is unlinked, the system requires more time, and does not always show accurate information, it does not support these goals.
comments_for_h8: When we use other systems (e.g., Access, REDCap), we are much more operational efficient.
comments_for_h10: 1CDP has caused significant duplication of datasets/analyses

i1: ["In developing and implementing our inquiry tracker, our biggest challenge was the time we had to create this. I believe there were constraints with our dedicated team. We ultimately had to make most of the decisions within weeks. We only had about 1 or 2 days to test the system before going live. That being said, once we went live, all issues had been resolved and addressed quickly, and the system works great. Design and implementation was just VERY fast and during a very difficult and overburdened time. ","Regarding our Jurisdictional Contact List-- this entire module was built for us during the shutdown and with 0 input from our team. Initially, then this was discussed, our team was envisioning a much more interactive interface that could link to existing inquiries, etc.. This was not what was produced, however its working well for us now that it's in production. It was also challenging that we were unaware of their work and they were using a far outdated contact list that then had to be rapidly edited/updated once the shutdown was over (including multiple deletions to entire columns, fields, entries, etc.). Again, we felt rushed with this, but understand the shutdown challenges were unprecedented.       "]
